title: सुदर्शनाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सुदर्शनाष्टकम् ॥

प्रतिभटश्रेणिभीषणं वरगुणस्तोमभूषणं
जनिभयस्थानतारणं जगदवस्थानकारणम्।
निखिलदुष्कर्मकर्शनं निगमसद्धर्मदर्शनं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ १॥

शुभजगद्रूपमण्डनं सुरगणत्रासखण्डनं
शतमखब्रह्मवन्दितं शतपथब्रह्मनन्दितम्।
प्रथितविदिक्पालवन्दितं प्रतितसन्तापखण्डनं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ २॥

निजपदप्रीतसद्गणं निरुपधिस्फीतसद्गुणं
निगमनिर्वाण्यवैभवं निजपरव्यूहवैभवम्।
हरिहयद्वेषिदारणं हरपुरप्लोषकारणं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ ३॥

स्फुटतटिज्जालपिञ्जरं द्रुघणवत्प्रौढवृत्तरं
शरणिगद्यन्तसत्फलं प्रणतदुःखाद्रिवज्रवत्।
हरनिरीक्षणस्वरूपं सकलभूपालशासनं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ ४॥

महितसम्पत्सदक्षरं विहितसम्पत्षडक्षरं
षडरचक्रप्रतिष्ठितं सकलतत्त्वप्रतिष्ठितम्।
विविधसंकल्पकल्पकं विबुधसंकल्पकल्पकं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ ५॥

प्रतिमुखालीढबन्धुरं प्रतिमुखानुग्रहान्दुरं
दितिजवृन्दप्रदारणं दिनकरकोटिसन्निभम्।
दिनदिनत्रासवारणं दनुजविस्तारदारणं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ ६॥

दुरधिगमस्वरूपगं विविधचित्राक्रियातिगं
विचित्रचित्रोद्भटक्रियं विमलचित्रोज्ज्वलद्दयम्।
सुरगणतापनाशनं शरणदीक्षितरक्षणं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ ७॥

प्रमितबन्धुप्रमोदनं प्रजित साक्षेपतोषणम्।
निजगुणव्यक्तमण्डनं निगमभव्यद्विजावनम्।
सकलसद्वस्तुचिन्तनं मनुजबन्धूत्तमावनं
जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनं जय जय श्रीसुदर्शनम्॥ ८॥

श्रीसुदर्शनाष्टकं यः पठेत् सततं नरः।
स सर्वदुःखविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वरक्षां लभेद्ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री वेदान्तदेशिकविरचितं श्री सुदर्शनाष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sudarshana Ashtakam ||

Pratibhata-shreni-bhishanam vara-guna-stoma-bhushanam
Jani-bhaya-sthana-taranam jagad-avasthana-karanam.
Nikhila-dushkarma-karshanam nigama-sad-dharma-darshanam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshnam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 1 ||

Shubha-jagad-rupa-mandanam sura-gana-trasa-khandanam
Shata-makha-Brahma-vanditam shata-patha-Brahma-nanditam.
Prathita-vidik-pala-vanditam pratita-santapa-khandanam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 2 ||

Nija-pada-prita-sad-ganam nirupadhi-sphita-sad-gunam
Nigama-nirvaanya-vaibhavam nija-para-vyuha-vaibhavam.
Hari-haya-dveshi-daranam hara-pura-plosha-karanam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 3 ||

Sphuta-tatij-jala-pinjaram drudhana-vat-praudha-vrittaram
Sharani-gadyanta-sat-phalam pranata-duhkha-adri-vajravat.
Hara-nirikshana-svarupam sakala-bhu-pala-shasanam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 4 ||

Mahita-sampat-sad-aksharam vihita-sampat-shad-aksharam
Shad-ara-chakra-pratishtitam sakala-tattva-pratishtitam.
Vividha-sankalpa-kalpakam vibudha-sankalpa-kalpakam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 5 ||

Prati-mukha-alidha-bandhuram prati-mukha-anugraha-anduram
Ditija-vrinda-pradaranam dina-kara-koti-sannibham.
Dina-dina-trasa-varanam danuja-vistara-daranam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 6 ||

Duradhigama-svarupa-gam vividha-chitra-akriya-atigam
Vichitra-chittra-udbhata-kriyam vimala-chitra-ujjvalad-dayam.
Sura-gana-tapa-nashanam sharana-dikshita-rakshanam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 7 ||

Pramita-bandhu-pramodanam prajita-sakshepa-toshanam.
Nija-guna-vyakta-mandanam nigama-bhavya-dvija-avanam.
Sakala-sad-vastu-chintanam manuja-bandhu-uttama-avanam
Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam Jaya Jaya Shri Sudarshanam. || 8 ||

Shri Sudarshana-ashtakam yah pathet satatam narah.
Sa sarva-duhkha-vinirmuktah sarva-raksham labhed-dhruvam.

|| Iti Shri Vedanta-Deshika-Virachitam Shri Sudarshana Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शत्रु-श्रेणियों को भयभीत करने वाले, श्रेष्ठ गुणसमूहों से भूषित, जन्म-भय-स्थान से तारण करने वाले, जगत् की स्थिति के कारणभूत, समस्त दुष्कर्मों को नष्ट करने वाले, वेदों के सद्धर्म दर्शन करने वाले — श्री सुदर्शन की जय हो, जय हो!॥ १॥

शुभ जगत् रूप को अलंकृत करने वाले, देवगणों के भय को खण्डित करने वाले, इन्द्र और ब्रह्मा द्वारा वन्दित, दिक्पालों द्वारा प्रणमित, सन्तापों को खण्डित करने वाले — श्री सुदर्शन की जय हो, जय हो!॥ २॥

हरि के शत्रुओं का दारण (विदारण) करने वाले, त्रिपुरासुर के नाश का कारण बने, विद्युत्-जाल से पिंजरित, वज्र के समान शरणागतों के दुःखपर्वत को तोड़ने वाले — श्री सुदर्शन की जय हो!॥ ३-४॥

कोटि सूर्यों के समान तेजस्वी, दैत्यसमूहों का विदारण करने वाले, प्रतिदिन भय का निवारण करने वाले — श्री सुदर्शन की जय हो!॥ ६॥

जो मनुष्य श्री सुदर्शनाष्टक का सतत पाठ करता है, वह सर्वदुःखों से विमुक्त होकर निश्चित रूप से सर्वरक्षा प्राप्त करता है॥
benefits: Composed by Vedanta Desika — one of the most powerful protection stotras
Sudarshana Chakra is Vishnu's supreme weapon that destroys all evil
Invokes absolute protection from enemies, black magic, and negative forces
Traditionally recited for removal of all dushkarma (accumulated bad karma)
The six-syllabled mantra (shadakshara) is embedded in this stotra
Invokes the effulgence and power of a million suns
Recited in Sri Vaishnava tradition for averting all dangers
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Sudarshana Ashtakam (सुदर्शनाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Vedanta Desika. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant absolute protection from enemies, black magic, and negative forces as well as grant the effulgence and power of a million suns. Additionally, it is known to composed by Vedanta Desika — one of the most powerful protection stotras. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar), Sunday (Ravivar), and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Sudarshana Jayanti and Ekadashi. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Sudarshana Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Sudarshana Ashtakam by Vedanta Desika in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses glorifying Vishnu's divine discus for supreme protection.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Sudarshana Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sudarshana Ashtakam was composed by Swami Vedanta Desika (1268-1369 CE), the great Vishishtadvaita acharya of the Sri Vaishnava sampradaya, who is said to have composed it at Tiruvahindrapuram (modern Cuddalore district, Tamil Nadu) where the Sudarshana Chakra is enshrined as Chakrathazhwar. Tradition records that Vedanta Desika composed the hymn to invoke the protective power of Lord Vishnu's discus during a period of plague and persecution, and that the very recitation drove away the affliction. The eight-verse hymn remains one of the most beloved compositions in Vedanta Desika's vast stotra corpus alongside Hayagriva Stotra, Garuda Dandakam and Daya Shatakam, and is enshrined as part of daily worship in Sri Vaishnava temples across South India. (Sources: Wikipedia Vedanta Desika; Hindupedia Sri Vaishnavism; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Sudarshana and what does the Ashtakam invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: Sudarshana is the personified discus (chakra) of Lord Vishnu, depicted with sixteen arms holding various weapons, surrounded by a flaming hexagonal yantra, and worshipped as Chakraperumal or Chakrathazhwar in Sri Vaishnava temples. He represents the protective and destructive power of the Lord — the executioner of asuras, the dispeller of darkness, and the supreme remover of all obstacles, diseases, hostile mantras and negative energies. The Sudarshana Ashtakam invokes him by names such as Pratibhata-shrenibhishana, Vinatasuta, Yamya-graha, Bhakta-jana-sankalpa-pati, calling on him to destroy enemies of the dharma, dispel negative entities, cure diseases, and protect the devotee from every direction. (Sources: Hindupedia Sudarshana; Wikipedia Chakrathazhwar; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of the Sudarshana Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: As an ashtakam the composition has eight main verses plus a phalashruti, each verse ending with the refrain Jaya Jaya Sri Sudarshana, Jaya Jaya Sri Sudarshana — a powerful repeated invocation that gives the hymn its distinctive musical character. The eight verses praise different aspects of Sudarshana: his terrifying form to enemies, his role as the personification of all weapons, his purifying flame, his blue-throated and lotus-eyed beauty, his destruction of bhuta-preta-pishacha, his command over the directional guardians, his support of the devotee's vows, and his ultimate identity with Vishnu's will. The phalashruti promises sweeping protection and the eight-fold prosperity (ashta-aishvarya). (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Sudarshana Ashtakam phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Sudarshana Ashtakam promises that one who recites it daily is protected from all enemies (visible and invisible), from the eight kinds of grahas (planetary and bhutic possessions), from black magic, abhichara, drishti-dosha (evil eye), and from all chronic and acute diseases. Material rewards include victory in legal matters, restoration of lost wealth, success in undertakings, and the eight-fold prosperity (ashta-aishvarya). Spiritual rewards include purification of the mind, fearlessness, devotion to Vishnu, and ultimately attainment of Vaikuntha. The phalashruti specifically notes that recitation during Sudarshana Homa multiplies the benefits and that even hearing the ashtakam protects pregnant women and small children. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Sudarshana Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday is traditionally the most powerful day for Sudarshana worship in the Sri Vaishnava calendar, drawing on the chakra's role as the conqueror of Saturn-related afflictions. Daily morning recitation after the snana and sandhya is the standard practice. Special occasions include every Ekadashi (especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi in Margashirsha), the annual Sudarshana Jayanti observed on different dates by different sampradayas, and the Sudarshana Homa periods conducted at Tiruvahindrapuram, Srirangam and other Divya Desams. Devotees facing serious illness, hostile environments, court cases, or negative supernatural influences are advised to recite the ashtakam 11, 21 or 108 times daily until relief manifests. (Sources: Hindupedia Sri Vaishnavism; Drikpanchang; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Sudarshana Ashtakam and Sudarshana Mantra Japa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sudarshana Mula Mantra is the eight-syllable bija-pradhana mantra Om Sudarshanaya Vidmahe Maha-jvalaya Dhimahi Tanno Chakrah Prachodayat (the Sudarshana Gayatri), or the longer Sudarshana Maha Mantra used in formal yantra-puja. These mantras require diksha from a qualified Vaishnava acharya before formal japa. The Sudarshana Ashtakam is a stotra — a praise hymn open to all devotees without initiation, suitable for daily recitation at home. Most practitioners begin with the ashtakam to develop bhakti and protective grace, then receive the mantra-diksha later for deeper sadhana. The two practices complement each other and are commonly combined during Sudarshana Homa. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Sudarshana Ashtakam be used during Sudarshana Homa?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. The Sudarshana Homa is one of the most powerful Vaishnava fire rituals, conducted to remove serious obstacles, chronic illness, planetary afflictions, hostile mantras and protective covering for an entire household or community. The Sudarshana Ashtakam is recited as a stuti-anga either before kindling the fire, between rounds of mantra-japa, or as the concluding namaskara after purnahuti. Major homas at Tiruvahindrapuram, Ahobilam Mutt and other Sri Vaishnava centres include the ashtakam in the standard liturgy. Even at home, devotees performing simpler Sudarshana puja with a chakra-yantra incorporate the ashtakam as the praise component flanking the mantra-japa. (Sources: Hindupedia Sudarshana Homa; Stotra Nidhi; Wikipedia Vedanta Desika.)
